The C ABI is the `machbus_session_*` surface (38 exported functions) over a single opaque `MachbusSession*` handle pinned at `MACHBUS_C_ABI_VERSION = 3`; the sans-IO loop is feed (`machbus_session_feed`) / tick (`machbus_session_tick`) / drain transmit (`machbus_session_poll_transmit`) / drain events (`machbus_session_poll_event`), with errors surfaced through `machbus_session_last_error`; `make bind-c-check` checks the generated `include/machbus.h` for drift, `make c-demo` builds `examples/c_abi/demo.c` and the `compile_surface.c`/`layout.c` compile-surface probes (every prototype exercised, C-header static layout assertions), and `make c-full-demo` builds and runs `examples/c_abi/full_demo.c`; add broader external-trace-backed C workflows.

The Python binding exposes a single `machbus.Session` class over the sans-IO `Session` core (advance timers with `Session.tick`, feed received CAN frames, drain transmit frames and events); `make python-demo` runs `examples/python_binding/basic.py` and the pytest-shaped dependency-free `examples/python_binding/regression.py` runner with golden expected values for constructor shape, claim state, diagnostic SPNs, event-drop accounting, subsystem flags, client guard/state methods, event subtypes, and helper clamp output; the Makefile gate also builds a wheel, installs it into a fresh disposable venv with pip install --no-deps, verifies metadata/export shape, and reruns the regression smoke against the non-editable wheel install; add a multi-interpreter wheel matrix if publishing beyond the local abi3 smoke.
